narrative.headline: What 2016 Honda Pilot owners report most
narrative.summary: Engine and electrical problems dominate owner complaints for this model, with the most common pattern being sudden engine shutdown while driving at highway speeds, accompanied by the entire instrument panel lighting up. Owners also report smoke emerging from under the hood in some cases. Powertrain and fuel system concerns round out the complaint picture, with some incidents involving safety-critical situations including passengers in the vehicle.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the engine shutting off without warning while driving at highway speeds, with the instrument panel illuminating all at once and smoke observed coming from the front driver side of the vehicle. · 7 fire · 4 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the entire instrument panel lighting up simultaneously while underway, often coinciding with engine shutdown and smoke visible from the front of the vehicle. · 5 fire · 3 crash

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report safety concerns with the powertrain that they describe as unresolved after contacting Honda, characterizing the manufacturer's response as reluctant and dismissive. · 5 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: Owners report the vehicle's computer displaying warning messages while traveling on the highway, in some cases with other passengers present in the vehicle. · 1 fire · 2 crash · 8 injured
